findings.evidence: The decisive office encounter is withheld at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:192, then reduced to recap at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:206 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:222.
findings.problem: The chapter ducks its most consequential coercive scene. Mohit's pressure campaign against June is the hinge on which the coalition breaks, but the text closes the office door and gives the reader the offer only as post-meeting paraphrase. Because the manipulation arrives secondhand, the chapter softens the exact moment bureaucratic harm becomes personal leverage. That is sanitization drift by summary replacing scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:194, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:206, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:222, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:238 so the chapter stages Mohit's pressure in real time rather than only paraphrasing it afterward. Keep Leela's close-third frame by using overheard fragments, June's exact recalled lines, or a brief embedded reconstruction anchored to June's bodily response, but the reader needs at least one concrete leverage move and June's immediate answer on the page.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:194 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:206 contains at least one directly rendered exchange in which Mohit ties June's future to disengaging from the grievance, plus a visible bodily consequence on June in the moment; fail if the offer and pressure still reach the reader only as generalized after-the-fact summary.

findings.evidence: The opening run from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:7 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:47 stays almost entirely external; the first full inward turn does not land until snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:49.
findings.problem: The chapter opens with a long sequence of dialogue and blocking before Leela's interior experience reasserts itself. In a close-third chapter, that many externally staged paragraphs in a row makes the scene read like coverage rather than pressure lived through a body. Ben's betrayal is important enough that Leela's shock, shame, or procedural recognition needs to shape the scene sooner.
findings.rewrite_direction: Seed two or three brief interior beats into snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:39, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:45 so Leela's thought, sensation, or self-assessment drives the opening instead of arriving late. Keep the beats costly and immediate rather than retrospective explanation.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised stretch between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:7 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:47 contains at least two explicit Leela interior beats before line 49 and no run longer than nine consecutive paragraphs in that span is purely external; fail if the scene still reads as dialogue coverage until snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:49.

findings.evidence: The same negation/correction sentence shape recurs at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:120, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:182, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:252, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:470, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:488.
findings.problem: Leela's narration keeps arriving through a `not X / the real thing is Y` pivot. Used once or twice, this self-correcting logic is sharp and character-true. At this density it becomes a visible sentence formula that pre-chews the interpretation instead of letting the image or judgment land cleanly.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast at least three of the cited beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:120, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:182, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:252, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:470, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:488 so the judgment lands positively and specifically without first negating an easier version.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least three of the cited passages no longer rely on a negation/correction pivot and still preserve the same dramatic meaning when read in place; fail if the revised chapter still stacks three or more `not X / actually Y` constructions across those same beats.

findings.evidence: Character descriptors recur as dossier refreshers at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:154, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:278, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:296, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:334.
findings.problem: The chapter over-reuses style-bible tells instead of generating fresh scene-specific observation. June's gum and jaw, Ben's expensive-jacket and too-young markers, and Omar's fear-in-the-jaw cue reappear often enough to feel loaded from a dossier rather than discovered under this chapter's pressure. That flattens characterization right when the chapter should be getting more singular.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least three of the cited beats in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:154, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:278, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:296, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:334 with fresh physical business or material detail unique to this crisis. Keep one signature tell if needed, but stop reintroducing each person from the style sheet every time they enter.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:154, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:296, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:334, at least three moments identify Ben, June, or Omar through new chapter-specific behavior or material detail rather than repeating the existing jacket, gum, or jaw shorthand; fail if those scenes still lean on the same stock tells to announce who is under pressure.

summary: Booker-level causality and dialogue texture mostly hold: Ben's betrayal, Mohit's reputational counterattack, and the coalition's fracture track cleanly, voices stay distinct, and I found no continuity contradiction, canonical-tense drift, or example-line reuse. The chapter still fails on craft/prose because it summarizes the decisive Mohit-June coercion instead of rendering it, lets the opening office sequence run too long without Leela's interiority, leans on repeated negation/correction sentence shapes, and refreshes character dossiers through recycled tells instead of fresher scene-bound observation.
